channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Police review writings tied to Nashville school shooter who killed female student and himself
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The investigation into why a Nashville high school student fatally shot a classmate before killing himself has centered on his online writings, which authorities describe as concerning warning signs.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Congressman says he believes aliens have underwater bases
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) said Wednesday he believes aliens have underwater bases on Earth. “I just think travelin’ light years, I think it happens. I think it's possible in the vastness of God's great universe.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally, calling it “blatantly unconstitutional” during the first hearing in a multi-state effort challenging the order.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Elton John, Diane Warren and more react to 2025 Oscar music nominations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The 2025 Oscar nominations are here, and its music categories reflect a wide range of talent. The often-nominated Diane Warren is represented for a 16th time for “The Journey” from “The Six Triple Eight.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LA wildfires: Surveillance video captures suspects stealing fire equipment during disaster response
item.description: Federal investigators are searching for two men who were caught on video stealing critical fire equipment during the Los Angeles wildfire response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump order ending federal DEI programs leaves agencies and stakeholders on uncertain ground
item.description: From federal agencies to stakeholders who get federal dollars for special training, many are trying to process how President Donald Trump’s sweeping executive order putting a stop to diversity, equity and inclusion programs across the U.S. government will upend their work.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, AYANNA ALEXANDER and COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2025 Oscar nominations: The biggest snubs and surprises
item.description: The members of the film academy voted to honor a few outliers — and overlook a couple of hopeful contenders — at the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, KRON4

item.title: Post Malone teams up with Oreo on limited-edition cookie flavor
item.description: Oreo has partnered with Post Malone for its newest cookie, featuring a first-of-its-kind filling for the brand.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Colorado DNA analyst appears on forgery charges as validity of more than 500 cases in doubt
item.description: GOLDEN, Colo. (AP) — A former Colorado Bureau of Investigation DNA scientist is facing 102 criminal charges, including dozens of counts of forgery, after authorities said she cut corners and didn't follow standard testing protocols in hundreds of criminal cases.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at churches. Now clergy are weighing how to resist
item.description: Numerous faith leaders across the U.S. say the immigration crackdown launched by President Donald Trump’s new administration has sown fear within their migrant-friendly congregations.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Court temporarily blocks Trump order on birthright citizenship
item.description: A federal judge temporarily blocked President Trump’s order seeking to end birthright citizenship for the children of many migrants, a major hit to one of the president’s Day 1 orders. Trump's order directly contradicts the Constitution, which guarantees citizenship for all people born within the U.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Virginia fugitive apprehended in Whitesboro, New York
item.description: Hasimba Brown is currently on Parole in the state of Virginia for a manslaughter conviction in the state of New York.
item.dc:creator: Josh McMullen, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s blizzard of orders gets pushback, questions from GOP lawmakers
item.description: President Donald Trump’s blizzard of executive orders during the first few days of his presidency has sent Republican lawmakers scrambling to make sense of what impact they’ll have on the country, and some GOP senators are already raising questions and concerns.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about President Donald Trump's order targeting the rights of transgender people
item.description: One of President Donald Trump's executive orders offers a new federal government definition of the sexes that could have a major impact on transgender people.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: Attorneys in the department's Civil Rights Division were ordered not to file any new complaints, amicus briefs or other certain court papers “until further notice," one of the memos said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Police review writings tied to Nashville school shooter who killed female student and himself
item.description: Authorities are examining “very concerning online writings and social media posts” connected to the shooter who killed a female student and wounded another student in a Nashville high school cafeteria.
item.dc:creator: Kristin M. Hall, Travis Loller And Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Over 50,000 under evacuation orders or warnings as wildfire imperils homes north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — More than 50,000 people were under evacuation orders or warnings Wednesday as a huge and fast-moving wildfire swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but fire officials said a rapid ground and air assault was giving them the upper hand.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen fatally shoots a female student and himself at Antioch High School in Nashville, police say
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A shooting in a Nashville high school cafeteria Wednesday left a female student dead and another student wounded, nearly two years after another deadly school shooting in the city that ignited an emotional debate about gun control in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIN M. HALL, TRAVIS LOLLER and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jury convicts serial killer truck driver in 2007 murder
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS — It took a Marion Superior Court jury less than 90 minutes to convict a serial killer truck driver of the murder of an Indianapolis woman more than 17 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Russ McQuaid, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs sues man who claimed to have incriminating sex tapes, says they didn't exist
item.description: Combs, 55, has pleaded not guilty to sex trafficking charges lodged against him after his September arrest.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former Arkansas officer arrested, charged with assault in beating of handcuffed inmate
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A former Arkansas police officer who was caught on video beating a handcuffed inmate in the back of his patrol car last year has been arrested and charged with aggravated assault.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A$AP Rocky's lawyers look for favorable jurors on charges he fired gun at former friend
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nearly a hundred jurors packed into a Los Angeles courtroom again on Wednesday with the possibility that they’ll serve on the trial of A$AP Rocky, who is charged with firing a gun at a former friend.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lynn Ban, star of Netflix reality show, dies at 52 after emergency surgery
item.description: Lynn Ban, who starred in Netflix's reality show "Bling Empire: New York," has died weeks after a skiing accident and emergency brain surgery.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, KRON4

item.title: Mobile Civic Center dome collapses from the weight of snow
item.description: MOBILE, Ala. (WKRG) - What was left of the Mobile Civic Center collapsed Wednesday morning due to the weight of the snow that fell Tuesday, officials said. According to city officials, the dome collapsed just after 11 a.m. and no injuries were reported.
item.dc:creator: Summer Poole, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Previously approved refugee flights to US canceled after Trump order
item.description: The U.S. refugee program is considered one of the most rigorous on the globe, providing a pathway to safe haven for those fleeing danger or who has faced persecution due to their race, religion, nationality or political views.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thousands of Vornado space heaters recalled over potential 'shock and fire' hazards
item.description: Thousands of Vornado space heaters sold on Amazon have been recalled amid concerns over possible "electric shock and fire hazards," according to the company.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump demands apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: President Donald Trump early Wednesday morning slammed the reverend at a National Cathedral prayer service for the inauguration who called on him to have mercy on transgender children and immigrant families.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown leaves some families weighing risk of sending kids to school
item.description: Fears intensified for some when the Trump administration announced Tuesday it would allow federal immigration agencies to make arrests at schools, churches and hospitals, ending a decades-old policy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cat mistakenly left in plane cargo hold makes 3 trips in 24 hours
item.description: A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s blanket Jan. 6 pardons stun Republicans on Capitol Hill
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping pardons of more than 1,500 people charged with crimes related to the Capitol riot of Jan. 6, 2021, including individuals who assaulted police officers, stunned Republican lawmakers who witnessed firsthand the chaos on Capitol Hill four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mittens the cat becomes an accidental frequent flyer after getting mistakenly left on a plane
item.description: A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man arrested at Utah nursing home decades after Hawaii murder
item.description: A man was arrested at a Utah nursing home on suspicion of second-degree murder in connection to a decades-old case from Hawaii.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Napuunoa, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WGN 9
